div.h3: Technical Implementation Spec: Syscall Overhead & Memory Ring Buffers

div.p: Architectural Summary: io_uring represents the transition from Readiness Notification (Reactive) to Completion Queues (Proactive). It eliminates the syscall tax by leveraging shared memory rings and asynchronous kernel workers.

ul.li: Context Switches: High. At least 2 per I/O (wait + read).
Data Transfer: Requires copy from kernel to user buffer during read().
Blockage: File I/O is problematic as epoll doesn't support regular files (always "ready").
Shared Memory: User and Kernel map same physical pages for SQ/CQ.
Memory Barriers: Required to sync head/tail without locks.
IORING_SETUP_SQPOLL: Enables true zero-syscall I/O by offloading submission to a kernel thread.
